candidates.name: lagged-asset-autocorrelation-regime-consistency

economic_rationale: The strategy combines trend and reversal signals. A credible composite should not require only persistent or only mean-reverting recent market dynamics to produce positive net returns.
failure_reasons: BTC-USDT positive-autocorrelation mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.070748981923
BTC-USDT nonpositive-autocorrelation mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.006471835651
ETH-USDT positive-autocorrelation mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.305102487835
hypothesis: BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT net rolling OOS returns have positive annualized arithmetic means in both positive and non-positive prior asset-return autocorrelation regimes.
limitations: BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT are development markets, not untouched holdouts.
Observed regime labels are treated as fixed during resampling, so regime-estimation uncertainty is not separately modeled.
Moving-block concatenation introduces artificial joins between observed blocks.
The analysis does not model spread, impact, capacity, latency, or partial fills.

method.acceptance_rule: all four 95% lower bounds must be strictly positive
method.autocorrelation_statistic: Pearson lag-1 autocorrelation within the prior 60 asset returns
method.nonpositive_regime_rule: lagged autocorrelation is less than or equal to zero
method.positive_regime_rule: lagged autocorrelation is greater than zero
method.resampling: paired non-circular moving blocks over observed regime and net-return rows
